evals.prompt: I'm building a REST API in Node.js for a mobile app. Users log in with email/password and should stay logged in for 30 days without re-entering credentials. What auth approach should I use and how do I implement it?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation for JWT with refresh token rotation (short-lived access token + long-lived refresh token stored server-side). Implementation of login endpoint, JWT middleware, refresh endpoint, and token rotation logic. Security notes about storage on mobile.
expectations: Recommends JWT with refresh tokens (not just long-lived JWTs)
Provides implementation of login endpoint returning access + refresh tokens
Mentions security considerations (token storage, rotation, revocation)
Code is complete and runnable Node.js/Express

evals.prompt: How do I implement RBAC in a FastAPI app? We have three roles: admin (full access), manager (can read/write but not delete), viewer (read only). Different API routes need different permission levels.
evals.expected_output: FastAPI RBAC implementation with role definition, permission mapping, a permission dependency, and examples of applying it to routes. Should show how to get current user from JWT and check their role.
expectations: Defines roles and their permissions (admin, manager, viewer)
Provides a FastAPI dependency for permission checking
Shows how to apply permission checks to route decorators
Provides a 403 Forbidden response for insufficient permissions
Includes an example of a protected route for each permission level
